Field.Label: Hue Lights Configuration

INSTRUCTIONS. You can: A. Add/modify/delete a bridge when you start you must first add (register) the plugin with the bridge later you can change bridge settings or delete a bridge ::: If you change any setting for a hue bridge, you must click on <Save> not <Cancel> at exit to fully save settings ::: B. Change general parameters C. Change debugging levels Comments: The bridges come in different hard and software version. bridge v2 supports API v1 bridge pro supports API v1 and v2 Make sure you set that up correctly. Set api in: add/delete/modify a bridge .. change api version Set bridge version in: add/delete/modify a bridge .. add or re-pair a new bridge "bridge" "hub" and "gateway" are used interchangeably
A. Change any aspect of a hue bridge (add/remove/modify):
Add/delete/modify a bridge:
Select action for the bridge:

List.Option: add or re-pair a new bridge

Field.Label: == enable /disable a bridge: Select bridge, select enable/disable:
Select a Hue Bridge to modify:
select enable/ disable for this bridge:
click to enable/disable
Field.Title: CONFIRM

Field.Label: == Modify API version of a bridge: Select bridge, select API version, click on CONFIRM:
Select a Hue Bridge to modify:
select API version for this bridge:

List.Option: api v 2 available on hue pro bridge

Field.Label: click to change API version#
Field.Title: CONFIRM

Field.Label: == Modify IP# of a bridge: Select bridge, enter new IP#, click on CONFIRM:
Select a Hue Bridge to modify:
Enter new IP address:
click to modify IP#
Field.Title: CONFIRM

Field.Label: == Delete a Hue Bridge: select bridge, click on Confirm:
Select a Hue Bridge to delete:
click to delete
Field.Title: CONFIRM

Field.Label: == Pair or (re-)pair a new or existing Hue Bridge with indigo, make sure you have selected the proper API version:

Field.tooltip: using bonjour the plugin will find new bridges on your network. This will take about: 3s + 4s*#of bridges = 7 secs for one bridge and 15s for 3 bridges. The plugin will do it in the background every 5 minutes automatically. With this button you can request to do it right now
Field.Label: Update the list of available Hue Bridges:
Field.Title: Exec search now

Field.Label: click on Exec search now if you have added a Hue Bridge, that has not been configured

Field.tooltip: Bridge number use 0 if you have just one. If you have several, repeat steps below for each after selection the Bridge number You must start with nummer 0
Field.Label: Select a Hue Bridge # for (re-) Pairing:
enter hub version (leave Blank to keep):
click to select:
Field.Title: CONFIRM

Field.Label: After click on CONFIRM the plugin will start to link to the bridge

AFTER CONFIRM use suggested IP# or Enter the IP# address manually: - or use your router info, - or you can obtain the Hue Bridge IP# address by registering on and using the www.meethue.com web site check your Hue Bridge settings. Cick on PHILIPS button on top of bridge The plugin will automatically try every 5s to pair with the IP# and bridge# It should work once you have pressed the button on the bridge

Field.tooltip: IP address of the Hue Bridge (e.g. 192.168.1.15. If the Bridge is given a different IP address by your router later on, you'll need to enter the new address here each time it changes.
Field.Label: IP Address of Hue Bridge (e.g. 192.168.1.15):

Field.tooltip: Before clicking this button, press the center button on the Hue hub. Within 30 seconds of doing that, come back here and click this button to pair with the Hue hub.
Field.Label: Start manual pairing process:
Field.Title: Execute

Field.Label: If the auto connect does not work press the <Execute> button

Field.tooltip: (read-only). Generated host ID assigned by the Hue Bridge to Hue Lights plugin. Provided here for troubleshooting purposes only.
Field.Label: Host ID:
(read-only). Generated host ID assigned by the Hue Bridge. Shown after successful pairing process
B. Other settings:

Field.tooltip: with this option checked the plugin will create a new indigo device as soon as a new light/sensor/group is created on a hue bridge. With the option OFF you can manually create new devices (add indigo device ...) Or you can use the menu item 'Add New Devices on Hue Bridge to Indigo' That will add all new devices that are on the hue bridge(s) in indigo
Field.Label: Auto create new Hue devices in Indigo
Directory for new indigo devices

Field.tooltip: used in mesaure # of secs since last message for api v2 event listener, if greater then restart listener
Field.Label: time wo events before restart of api-V2 listener:

List.Option: 1 minute if you have a lot of sensors amd movements

Field.tooltip: scale factor for light, sensors, groups: set higher if you have cpu load issues, lower if you like faster response time, default: sensors every 1 sec, lights,groups every 5 secs, other = every 3..5 minutes fixed use the largest for api v2 bridges
Field.Label: How often the Hue Bridge should be read (http apv1 calls):

List.Option: lights every 20 secs, sensors 8 secs, very slow response to changes
lights = every 1 secs, sensors every .5 secs

Field.Label: For bridge V2 (not Pro) in the plugin loop device are polled from the bridge(s) every sensors: 2 secs, lights and groups: 5 secs with this scale factor you can make that more frequent or less frequent others: fixed at 3-5 minutes
Tag for sensor Names:(Hue_THIS_br#_id_NameOnBridge)
Use for prefix for switches:(Hue_THIS_br#_id_NameOnBridge)
Use for prefix for lights:(Hue_THIS_br#_id_NameOnBridge)
Use for prefix for groups:(Hue_THIS_br#_id_NameOnBridge)

Field.tooltip: Enter how many Preset memories you'd like Hue Lights to keep saved (a number from 1 to 100). See warning below.
Field.Label: Preset Memories:
Enter how many Preset memories you'd like Hue Lights to keep saved (a number from 1 to 100). See warning below.

WARNING: Lowering the Preset Memories number will cause the highest Preset memory spaces to be permanently deleted. All actions that reference deleted Presets will result in Indigo errors when attempting to recall those Presets. Don't lower the number unless you're sure nothing uses the Presets that will be deleted.
C. Logging settings:

Field.tooltip: you can set to NO = do not log anything, yes = log everything or leave it to device = edit each dev and set log level there
Field.Label: Log changes received from Hue Bridge:
Log changes, set default for NEW devices:

Field.tooltip: either indigo log AND plugin.log or only in plugin.log file
Field.Label: Log messages in:

List.Option: ONLY in plugin.log file in the indigo log directory

Field.Label: Set debug areas ...
show logging test at startup:
Debug... ... (1) Init:
... (3) Edit indigo devs config etc:
... (4) Read From Bridge, write to plugin.log file:
... (5) Send Commands To Bridge:
... (6) Update Indigo Devices:
... (7) find Hue bridges:
... (8) event api v2:
... (9) write data read from bridge to prefs dir:
... (10) print network stats once a day:
... (11) special debug (only for development):
... (12) special debug ( starting for debug):
... (99) all of the above .. everything:
for experts only ! serach string for commands to find hue bridges
Use "/usr/bin/dns-sd -B _hue._tcp local." to look for hue tag, could be Hue Bridge or Philips ...
String to look for in finding hue bridges
